hero.headline: Unbought. Unbossed. Unanointed.
hero.subheadline: Democracy cannot be preserved nationally if it is subverted locally. Join the multiracial working-class coalition to take back IL-04.
hero.context: When Rep. García retired, party bosses handed his seat to his former chief of staff, who needed just 697 signatures. As an independent democratic socialist backed by the Chicago DSA, Byron must collect over 10,816 valid signatures to even appear on the ballot. We need your help to overcome a system rigged 15-to-1 against independent candidates.

bio.label: Meet the Organizer
bio.headline: From Ecuador to the City Council: A Life of Fighting Back
bio.p1: Byron Sigcho-Lopez emigrated alone from Ecuador at age 17, working his way through school to become a public school teacher in the very communities he would later serve. When the city moved to close 50 public schools in Black and Latino neighborhoods, Byron didn't write a letter. He organized.
bio.p2: As 25th Ward Alderman and a proud democratic socialist endorsed by Chicago DSA, Byron has taken on the Chicago political establishment head-on: passing anti-displacement legislation to protect Pilsen and Little Village families, standing with striking workers, and rejecting every dollar of corporate PAC money. He has never been bought, and he never will be.

housing.title: Housing as a Human Right
housing.desc: Stop displacement. Fight for rent control. Build truly affordable housing, not luxury condos rebranded as 'mixed-income.'

economic.title: Economic Populism
economic.desc: Tax the rich. Cap property taxes for working families. End corporate welfare and invest in the neighborhoods that have been disinvested for decades.

labor.title: Labor & Public Education
labor.desc: Stand with the CTU and every union fighting for dignity. Fully fund public schools. Stop the privatization pipeline that guts working-class communities.

environment.title: Environmental Justice
environment.desc: Hold toxic polluters accountable. No more sacrifice zones in Black and Brown neighborhoods. Clean air, clean water for everyone, not just the wealthy.

anointment.label: Why Independent?
anointment.headline: The Anointment
anointment.text: When Rep. Jesús "Chuy" García announced his retirement, he didn't give voters a choice. He filed for a fifth term, then moments before the filing deadline, his former chief of staff filed nominating petitions collected in just days. García withdrew and endorsed her, handing the seat to an uncontested successor. She needed only 697 signatures. Independent candidates must collect over 10,816. This is not democracy. This is an anointment.

footer.ticker_text: Average donation: $27. No corporate PAC money. Ever.
footer.paid_for: Paid for by Byron for IL-04. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

meet.hero_sub: From public school teacher to unapologetic democratic socialist Alderperson, Byron Sigcho-Lopez has spent his life fighting alongside the working class.
meet.origin_headline: From Ecuador to Chicago: Forged by Struggle
meet.origin_p1: Byron Sigcho-Lopez was born and raised in Quito, Ecuador. At 17, after a tragic car accident left his mother in a coma, his family insisted he leave the country to finish his education. He arrived in Tennessee alone, knowing no one and speaking no English. A generous local family took him in and helped him pursue an education.
meet.origin_p2: Byron earned a soccer scholarship, received an honorable mention for the All-State Tennessee Soccer Team, and graduated from Cumberland University with a B.A. in Mathematics and Business Administration, earning the prestigious President's Award. After graduation, he moved to Chicago and settled in Pilsen to start his life.
meet.awakening_p1: In Chicago, Byron became an adult education teacher at the University of Illinois at Chicago and founded UIC's Center for Literacy Spanish literacy program, making the GED accessible to Spanish-speaking immigrants. He also volunteered as a youth soccer coach at Pilsen Academy and earned his Master's in Economics from UIC.
meet.awakening_p2: As a lead researcher at UIC, he collaborated with the Chicago Teachers Union to investigate the financial relationships between charter school networks, school funding, and Chicago's political elite. He organized alongside parents and teachers like Karen Lewis to fight for fully funded public schools.
meet.awakening_p3: When Rahm Emanuel announced the closure of over 130 Chicago Public Schools, the largest mass school closing in American history, Byron stepped up. He joined the Pilsen Alliance and eventually served as its director for a decade, organizing community meetings, leading protests, and spearheading referendums to fight gentrification, displacement, and corruption.
meet.record_desc: In 2019, Byron ran a grassroots campaign to unseat the entrenched, corrupt Democratic machine in the 25th Ward. He committed to refuse political contributions from developers and big corporations, a commitment he has upheld to this day. Since taking office, he has transformed the ward office into one that serves all residents with care and dignity.
meet.corruption_desc: Took disgraced former Alderman Danny Solis to the Illinois Supreme Court and won changes to campaign finance rules, stopping indicted politicians from using campaign funds for legal defense. Strengthened the Office of Inspector General and continues to advocate for publicly financed elections.
meet.housing_desc: Directly enabled the creation and preservation of over 800 affordable housing units. Fought to buy the largest vacant site in Pilsen for affordable development, passed legislation protecting homeowners from predatory developers, and doubled the Affordable Requirements Ordinance minimum from 10% to 20%.
meet.environment_desc: Joined the hunger strike to #StopGeneralIron, fought MAT Asphalt's environmental racism in McKinley Park, and demanded accountability for the disastrous Hilco implosion. Led the effort to include environmental justice impact in city procurement contracts.
meet.workers_desc: Secured over $1 million in school infrastructure improvements, stood with the CTU for fully funded public schools, and walked picket lines with unionizing workers across the city. Defended teachers facing retaliation for fighting environmental racism and championed the Rudy Lozano Library expansion.
meet.value_frontlines_desc: Byron is a workhorse, not a show horse. He is an ideologically committed democratic socialist who is never afraid to be on the front lines. From hunger strikes for environmental justice to standing with striking workers, he shows up when it counts.
meet.value_nocorpmoney_desc: Since 2019, Byron has refused to take political contributions from large corporations or real estate developers. He has upheld this commitment every single day in office. His campaign is powered entirely by small-dollar donations from working people.
meet.value_accessible_desc: Every Tuesday night, including throughout the pandemic, Byron hosts Ward Night where any resident can personally meet with him, have their concerns heard, and receive city services. This is what representation looks like.
meet.value_family_desc: Byron lives in Pilsen with his wife Loreen and their three children. He is not a careerist passing through. He is a neighbor, a father, and a lifelong member of the community he fights for.
meet.cta_headline: Send an Unbought Organizer to Congress.

issues.hero_headline: A Platform for Working People, Not the Billionaire Class.
issues.hero_sub: Byron is fighting to guarantee a baseline standard of dignity for working families, seniors, teachers, and marginalized groups across IL-04.
issues.housing_1: Fight for rent stabilization and lift the ban on rent control in Illinois.
issues.housing_2: Protect seniors and longtime residents from predatory developers and property tax spikes.
issues.housing_3: Expand the Low Income Housing Trust Fund to be accessible for undocumented residents, as Byron successfully advocated for in Chicago.
issues.housing_4: Continue developing community-driven affordable housing, like the 280+ unit site at 18th and Peoria.
issues.economic_1: Implement a LaSalle Street tax to ensure the wealthiest financial institutions pay their fair share, ending the subsidy paid by working families.
issues.economic_2: Establish a public bank to give small businesses and residents fair access to credit.
issues.economic_3: Stand with unionizing workers, including direct support for Amazon distribution workers demanding humane conditions.
issues.education_1: Stand uncompromisingly with the Chicago Teachers Union and fight for a fully funded, democratically elected school board.
issues.education_2: Bring federal dollars to neighborhood schools, expanding on Byron's record of securing over $1 million in infrastructure improvements for schools like Benito Juarez Community Academy.
issues.environment_1: Hold toxic polluters accountable for environmental racism in working-class communities, continuing the fight against disasters like the Hilco implosion and MAT Asphalt.
issues.environment_2: Reopen the Department of the Environment to institutionalize climate change resiliency and address public health crises like asthma and heart disease.
issues.safety_1: Implement community-driven, block-by-block interventions to address the root causes of violence, rather than relying on failed status-quo models.
issues.safety_2: Ensure Chicago remains a reproductive health sanctuary city and expand funding for LGBTQIA+ inclusive social services.
issues.unbought_text: Byron completely rejects corporate PAC money and contributions from large real estate developers. He only answers to the working-class coalition of the 4th District. His campaign is funded by small-dollar donations from neighbors, teachers, union members, and working families. This is what it means to be truly independent.
issues.cta_headline: Fight for This Platform. Sign the Petition.

machine.hero_sub: We are building an independent, working-class movement to defeat a corrupt political machine that believes it can govern without the consent of the people.
machine.movement_intro: Byron Sigcho-Lopez is an ideologically committed democratic socialist and a firebrand who has spent his entire career fighting for the multiracial working class. He is not a politician looking for a promotion. He is an organizer who was called to serve by his community.
machine.unbought_text: Unlike establishment politicians, Byron completely rejects corporate PAC money and contributions from real estate developers. His campaign is powered entirely by thousands of small-dollar grassroots donations from teachers, nurses, union members, and working families. He has upheld this commitment since his first campaign in 2015 and has never wavered.
machine.tested_text: Byron's unique power comes from bottom-up organizing. As 25th Ward Alderperson, he passed anti-displacement legislation to protect homeowners from predatory developers, created over 800 units of affordable housing, stood uncompromisingly with the Chicago Teachers Union, doubled the Affordable Requirements Ordinance minimum, and joined a hunger strike to fight environmental racism. These are not campaign promises. These are receipts.
machine.pullquote: "I have never taken a dollar from a corporation or a developer. I never will. My loyalty is to the people of this district, not to the machine."
machine.local_p1: When Rep. Chuy Garcia decided to retire from Congress, he did not announce it publicly and let the voters decide. Instead, he waited until the final hours before the filing deadline. This ensured that only one person, his former Chief of Staff Patty Garcia, could get on the ballot with just 697 signatures. The seat was handed over in a backroom, not won in an election.
machine.local_p2: This maneuver earned a rare reprimand from the U.S. House Ethics Committee for "election subversion." While the machine's candidate relies on institutional inertia, frontline PACs, and a rigged filing system, Byron is building a grassroots base one doorstep at a time, funded entirely by working people.
machine.thesis: "Democracy cannot be preserved nationally if it is subverted locally."
machine.timeline_intro: What happened in IL-04 is not an isolated incident. It is part of a broader, dangerous pattern of the establishment insulating itself from the working class.
machine.timeline_2016: The DNC weaponized the undemocratic superdelegate system to block progressive momentum and override the will of grassroots voters.
machine.timeline_2020: The party establishment swiftly coalesced in a coordinated effort to block Bernie Sanders' grassroots movement, consolidating behind a single candidate overnight.
machine.timeline_2024: The party suppressed primary challenges and skipped debates, culminating in a late Biden drop-out and an uncompetitive process that left voters demoralized and demobilized.
machine.timeline_now: In IL-04, the same playbook is being used at the local level, handing a Congressional seat to a handpicked successor without a competitive election.
machine.fight_p1: The machine is not just trying to outspend Byron. It is actively trying to keep him off the ballot entirely. Illinois election law is designed to punish independent candidates. While the machine's anointed candidate needed only 697 signatures to get on the ballot, Byron must collect exactly 10,816 valid signatures.
machine.fight_p2: But it gets worse. The establishment will use bad-faith legal challenges to try and strike his petition signatures. Trained operatives will comb through every sheet, challenging every misspelled name, every address that is one digit off. To survive these challenges, the campaign must gather an overwhelming 40,000 to 50,000 signatures.
machine.stat_needed: Signatures actually needed to survive legal challenges
machine.fight_framing: Signing this petition is not just a legal formality. It is an act of defiance against a system designed to suppress independent voices. Every signature is a direct challenge to the machine's grip on power.
machine.cta_headline: Defy the Machine. Join the Movement.

volunteer.hero_headline: We Are The Machine's Worst Nightmare.
volunteer.hero_sub: We don't have corporate PAC money or backroom bosses. We have you. Join the multiracial working-class coalition to take back IL-04.
volunteer.blitz_p1: Illinois election law intentionally punishes independent candidates to protect the two-party duopoly. The campaign is legally mandated to collect exactly 10,816 valid signatures just to appear on the ballot, while his machine-backed opponent bypassed this requirement entirely with just 697.
volunteer.blitz_p2: The political establishment will inevitably deploy armies of lawyers and bad-faith challenges to strike valid signatures. The campaign urgently needs volunteers to help gather a massive surplus of 40,000 to 50,000 signatures to survive these attacks.
volunteer.canvass_desc: Knock on doors in Pilsen, Little Village, and the inner-ring suburbs to have meaningful, one-on-one conversations about housing, economic justice, and the fight against the machine. This is how movements are built: at the doorstep.
volunteer.ticket_desc: As an independent, Byron will not benefit from the straight-ticket Democratic vote. We need educators to teach voters how to split their ticket in November to defy the machine and cast a vote for a real representative.
volunteer.digital_desc: Reach voters where they are through rapid-response text campaigns, phone calls, and social media. This work can be done from anywhere, anytime, and is critical for expanding the movement beyond what door-knocking alone can achieve.
volunteer.signup_desc: Fill out the form and our team will reach out with next steps. Whether you have one hour or one hundred, there is a role for you in this movement.
volunteer.help_legend: How would you like to help?
volunteer.check_bilingual: Bilingual Outreach (I speak Spanish / Yo hablo español)
volunteer.compliance: By submitting, you agree to receive periodic campaign updates via SMS. Message and data rates may apply. Text STOP to unsubscribe.
volunteer.cta_headline: Can't Volunteer Today? Fund the Fight.
volunteer.cta_text: Every dollar helps buy clipboards, literature, and digital tools for the volunteer army. This campaign is entirely unbought and funded by working people like you.
